question: [File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: serveSpec()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly request the publicly accessible YAML spec endpoint (GET /api/v1/${config.openapi.swaggerUIPath}/${config.openapi.specFileName}.yml) with no authentication required, causing the server to transmit the full YAML document on every request with no response-level caching, resulting in sustained CPU serialization and network I/O overhead exceeding 30% above baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: serveSpec()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of HTTP cache-control headers (no ETag, no Last-Modified, no Cache-Control: max-age) on the serveSpec() handler to force the server to re-read and re-transmit the full YAML file on every request, preventing client-side or proxy-level caching and sustaining elevated I/O load?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: serveSwaggerDocs()] Can an unprivileged external user load the swagger UI endpoint (GET /api/v1/${config.openapi.swaggerUIPath}) repeatedly, causing swaggerUi.serve middleware to serve multiple large static JS/CSS assets per page load, and swaggerUi.setup() to re-render the full OpenAPI document on each request, driving sustained CPU and bandwidth consumption above 30% of baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getV1OpenApiFile()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated cold-start scenarios where v1OpenApiFile is undefined (e.g., by exploiting process restart or module reload conditions) causing fs.readFileSync() to perform synchronous disk I/O on every new worker process, blocking the Node.js event loop and degrading throughput by more than 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getOpenApiSpecObject()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated calls to getOpenApiSpecObject() by exploiting a code path where yaml.load() is invoked on the full YAML spec string without the v1OpenApiDocument cache being populated, causing repeated CPU-intensive YAML deserialization and increasing node CPU usage above 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: openApiValidator()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests to all documented API endpoints simultaneously, causing the express-openapi-validator middleware (loaded via openApiValidator()) to perform full schema validation on every response (when validateResponses is enabled), driving CPU usage above 30% of baseline through repeated JSON Schema validation cycles?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: serveSwaggerDocs()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that serveSwaggerDocs() registers both a .yml and .yaml spec endpoint (two separate GET routes serving the same large file) to double the effective bandwidth consumption per attacker request cycle, sustaining elevated network I/O above 30% of baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rest/middleware/openapiHandler.js -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: getOpenApiParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a request path that causes getOpenApiParameters() to chain .filter(p => p.$ref?.includes(OPEN_API_PARAMETER_LOCATION)).map(...).map(...).filter(...).map(...) over a large parameters array on every request, causing repeated O(n) CPU work per request and sustaining elevated CPU consumption above 30%?
